bird.name: Bewick's wren
bird.summary: Bewick's wren (Thryomanes bewickii) is a wren native to North America. It is the only species placed in the genus Thryomanes.

locations.name: 约翰·詹姆斯·奥杜邦
locations.summary: 约翰·詹姆斯·奥杜邦（英語：John James Audubon；法語：Jean-Jacques Audubon；1785年4月26日—1851年1月27日），美国画家、博物学家，法裔美國人，他绘制的鸟类图鉴《美国鸟类》被称作“美国国宝”…

locations.name: 美国鸟类 (书)
locations.summary: 《美国鸟类》（英語：The Birds of America），美国博物学家、画家约翰·詹姆斯·奥杜邦编著的一本书。书中，他绘制并详细描述了生活在美国的各种鸟类。 这本书是一系列书的合集，于1827年到1838年间第一次出版。其中一个副本…

relations.subject: Bewick's wren
relations.evidence: Eastern populations, prior to their decline, used to migrate from its northern range to the Gulf Coast. The preferred habitat of Bewick's wren is that of arid open woodlands and brush-filled areas such as hillsides and uplands, but will reside in humid areas locally (Subtropical and Temperate zones). They are more common than house wrens in drier habitats, such as those found in the Southwest. In California, Bewick's wrens inhabit a shrubland area called chaparral
relations.object_summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

relations.subject: Bewick's wren
relations.evidence: Despite this classification, no breeding pairs of Bewick's wrens are known to remain in Maryland. In 2014, the North American Bird Conservation Initiative placed the eastern Bewick's wren on its watch list. Several theories have been proposed to explain its decline in its eastern range, including pesticide use and competition from other bird species. The most likely reason seems to be competition from house wrens
relations.object_summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

relations.subject: Bewick's wren
relations.evidence: House wrens will destroy both the nests and eggs of Bewick's wrens. The reforestation of once open land has also negatively impacted the eastern Bewick's wrens. In California, habitat loss due to development has impacted Bewick's wren
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
